section.heading: Grants to improve supply, stability, safety, and training of agricultural labor force

content.p: In this section, the term “eligible entity” means an entity described in section 2008q(a) 11 See References in Text note below. of this title.

To assist agricultural employers and farmworkers by improving the supply, stability, safety, and training of the agricultural labor force, the Secretary may provide grants to eligible entities for use in providing services to assist farmworkers who are citizens or otherwise legally present in the United States in securing, retaining, upgrading, or returning from agricultural jobs.

paragraph.heading: Eligible services
paragraph.chapeau: The services referred to in paragraph (1) include—

subparagraph.content: the provision of agricultural labor market information;
short-term housing while in transit to an agricultural worksite;
workplace literacy and assistance with English as a second language;
health and safety instruction, including ways of safeguarding the food supply of the United States; and
such other services as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

content.p: Not more than 15 percent of the funds made available to carry out this section for a fiscal year may be used to pay for administrative expenses.

subsection.heading: Authorization of appropriations
subsection.chapeau: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section—

paragraph.content: such sums as are necessary for each of fiscal years 2008 through 2013; and
$10,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2014 through 2018.

note.heading: References in Text
note.p: Section 2008q(a) of this title, referred to in subsec. (a), was repealed by Pub. L. 115–334, title VI, § 6601(a)(1)(E), Dec. 20, 2018, 132 Stat. 4775.

note.heading: Codification
note.p: Pub. L. 110–234 and Pub. L. 110–246 enacted identical sections. Pub. L. 110–234 was repealed by section 4(a) of Pub. L. 110–246.
Section was enacted as part of the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008, and not as part of the Consolidated Farm and Rural Development Act which comprises this chapter.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 2014—Subsec. (d). Pub. L. 113–79 amended subsec. (d) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “There are authorized to be appropriated such sums as are necessary to carry out this section for each of fiscal years 2008 through 2012.”

note.heading: Effective Date
note.p: Enactment of this section and repeal of Pub. L. 110–234 by Pub. L. 110–246 effective May 22, 2008, the date of enactment of Pub. L. 110–234, see section 4 of Pub. L. 110–246, set out as a note under section 8701 of this title.

note.heading: Definition of “Secretary”
note.p: “Secretary” as meaning the Secretary of Agriculture, see section 8701 of this title.
